_readme.purpose: 写作评分链路防退化门语料 —— 12 篇评分锚 + 11 个对抗探针。配套脚本 scripts/scoring-gate.mjs 直调生产评分链路(判分锚 v3 prompt + 三路取中位)跑出「过/不过」，防止改 prompt/parse/calibration/中位逻辑后评分质量静默退化。
_readme.origin: 2026-07-12 判分锚 v3 大修 + 三路取中位验收 harness 固化。锚文来源：3 篇 ETS 官方带分样文(data/writingScoring/etsGoldenSamples.json 金标) + 7 篇内部校准样(scripts/calibration-test.js) + 2 篇作者自建 email 锚(补 email 2/5 分段空档)。探针为对抗攻击面(模板/跑题/连接词沙拉/过短/缺goal/注水/注入×2/复述题干/官方5分文+3错字)。
_readme.leaked: ets-disc-5-airplane、ets-disc-4-lightbulb 是评分 prompt 内的 few-shot 泄漏锚(模型见过原文)，clean 命中率统计时排除这两篇。
_readme.stability: ets-disc-5-vaccine、cal-email-4-heating 为稳定性文：中位流程整体跑 3 遍，极差 ≤0.5 判 stable。
_readme.acceptance: 见 docs/eval-spec/writing-scoring.md 的 6 条验收线。语料只读，改评分逻辑不动本文件。真实用户对照组(含用户作文)因隐私不落库，那是研究用对照非验收线。

professor.name: Dr. Achebe
professor.text: When people are asked about the most important discoveries or inventions made in the last two hundred years, they usually mention something very obvious, like the computer or the cell phone. But there are thousands of other discoveries or inventions that have had a huge impact on how we live today. What scientific discovery or technological invention from the last two hundred years—other than computers and cell phones—would you choose as being important? Why?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I mean, we're so used to science and technology that we are not even aware of all the things we use in our daily lives. I would probably choose space satellites. This technology happened in the last hundred years, and it has become important for so many things. Just think about navigation, or telecommunications, or even the military.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I am thinking about medical progress. Like, for example, when scientists discovered things about healthy nutrition. I am thinking of identifying all the vitamins we need to stay healthy. I am not sure exactly when the vitamin discoveries happened, but I know they are very important. Our health is much better than it was 200 years ago.

anchors.response: For me, the invention of the airpline, by brazilian inventor Santos Dumont, is a good example of a important invention from the last two centuries.It made possible to connect different places even if they are thousand of miles away one from another.People can be in any place of the world within twenty four hours or less from the time they get inside an airplane.This is fantastic!Also, the world trade become more efficient. You can eat fresh lobsters from northeastern Brazil even if you are in Dubai!I think that is really interesting.Since the ancient Greece the man wanted to fly like the birds, and Mr Dumont made it possible.And I am partiullary proud of him because he was a brazilian citizen, just like me.So, thats my opinion, I could show you some of my admiration for this great man and his beautiful invention.

anchors.response: From my personal point of view, I think the most important invention is the light bulb. Before it was invented, people had have to use candles for illumination in the evening. professor.name: Professor Diaz
professor.text: What is the most important invention in history?

students.name: Student A
students.text: I think the internet is the most important invention.

students.name: Student B
students.text: I believe the printing press changed the world most.

anchors.response: While both the internet and printing press transformed communication, I would argue that the airplane represents the most significant invention in history. Before aviation, international trade and cultural exchange were limited to those who could afford lengthy sea voyages. The development of commercial flight by figures like Santos-Dumont democratized global travel, enabling millions to cross borders that were previously impassable. Furthermore, aviation catalyzed advances in materials science and engineering that led to innovations in other fields. Although the internet connects us digitally, the airplane first connected us physically, laying the groundwork for the globalized world we inhabit today.

promptData.scenario: Your apartment's heating system stopped working last week. You have already tried adjusting the thermostat, but the radiators remain cold.
promptData.direction: Write an email to Mr. Harris. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Heating system problem
goals: Describe the problem with the heating system.
Explain how the problem has affected your daily life.
Request a specific repair arrangement or solution.

anchors.response:
Dear Mr. Harris,
I am writing to inform you about a problem about the heating system in my apartment. I moved in recently, and since last week, the heater has completely stop working. No matter how I adjust the thermostat, the radiators is still cold, and the temperature in the apartment has dropped a lot, especially at night.
This issue has starting to affect my daily life and study routine in a big way. Because my apartment is so cold, I have trouble to concentrate on my coursework, and I often need to stop studying early just for warm up under blankets. I also have difficulty to sleep well, which makes me feeling tired and less productive during the day.
Because of these effects, I would like to request that a repair technician can be sent to check and fix the heating system as soon as possible, ideally in the next two or three days. If the full repair will take more longer, I will appreciate if you can give me a temporary space heater so I can continue my daily activities comfortable.
Thank you for your attention on this matter. I look forward for your response soon.
Best regards,
Lisa

promptData.scenario: You are a subscriber of the poetry magazine Verse & Voice. Last week you tried to submit a poem through the magazine's online form and the page showed an error message.
promptData.direction: Write an email to the editor. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Poem submission question
goals: Express your appreciation for the magazine.
Describe the technical issue you experienced.
Ask about the status of your submission.

anchors.response:
Dear Ms. Rowe,

I am writing to explain a problem I had with the online submission form. I am a subscriber of Verse & Voice, and I really enjoy reading your magazine.

I tried to submit a poem through your online form last week. After I clicked the Submit button, the page showed an error message. I am not sure whether my poem was successfully received or not.

Could you please let me know if my submission went through? If not, I would be happy to submit it again.

Sincerely,
Zishuo

promptData.scenario: You attended a guest lecture by Dr. Thompson on campus last week and found it inspiring.
promptData.direction: Write an email to Dr. Thompson. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Thank you for your lecture
goals: Thank Dr. Thompson and mention a specific point from the lecture.
Explain how the lecture relates to your own interest.
Ask for brief advice or recommended resources.

anchors.response:
Dear Dr. Thompson,

I am writing to thank you for the guest lecture you gave on campus last week. I really enjoyed your talk and this point left a strong impression on me.

I am a senior student and I am choosing a topic for my thesis. After listening to your lecture, I started to think about changing my topic to marine biology, because it connects to my interest.

I would like to ask if you would be willing to give me some brief advice or recommend resources.

Sincerely,
Zishuo

promptData.scenario: You recently stayed at the Lakeside Hotel for a three-night conference trip. The room was excellent, but the promised airport shuttle never arrived.
promptData.direction: Write an email to the hotel manager. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Feedback on my recent stay
goals: Describe what you enjoyed about your stay.
Explain the problem with the airport shuttle.
Request an appropriate resolution.

anchors.response:
Dear Ms. Bennett,

I am writing to share some feedback about my three-night stay at the Lakeside Hotel during last week's conference. Overall, my experience was excellent: the room was spotless, the staff at the front desk were consistently attentive, and the quiet lakeside view made it easy to prepare for my sessions each morning.

There was, however, one significant problem. I had reserved the complimentary airport shuttle for my arrival, and I confirmed the booking by email the day before. When my flight landed, no shuttle appeared, and after waiting for nearly an hour I had to pay for a taxi to reach the hotel.

I would be grateful if you could look into what went wrong with the shuttle reservation and reimburse the taxi fare, which came to forty dollars. I would happily return for future trips, and a clear response would reassure me that the booking system can be relied upon.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Sincerely,
Zishuo

promptData.scenario: Your library book is overdue because the library's online renewal page was not working.
promptData.direction: Write an email to the librarian. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Overdue book
goals: Describe the problem you had renewing the book.
Explain why you could not return it on time.
Ask what you should do now.

anchors.response: Hi. My book is late. The website not work so I can not renew. Sorry about that. What I do now? Thank you.

probes.note: 万能模板套话,零题目实质接触

professor.name: Dr. Achebe
professor.text: We've been discussing whether social media has improved or harmed public debate. Some argue it gives ordinary people a voice and spreads information quickly, while others believe it amplifies misinformation and outrage. Do you think social media has been good or bad for public debate? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe social media has improved public debate because it lets people who were once ignored share their views and organize around issues they care about.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, social media has harmed debate because outrage spreads faster than facts, and algorithms reward the most extreme takes rather than the most thoughtful ones.

probes.text: This is a very important and interesting topic that people have debated for many years. There are two sides to every issue, and both sides make good points that we should consider carefully. In my opinion, it depends on the situation and the person. Some people think one way, and other people think another way. We should always look at all the different perspectives before making a decision. In conclusion, this is a complex issue with no easy answer, and everyone should think about it carefully and respect other opinions.

probes.note: 语法完美但完全不回应教授问题/学生观点(写成美食游记)

probes.text: Last summer I travelled through the coastal towns of southern Italy, and the experience reshaped my understanding of regional cuisine. Each morning I would wander to a local market, where vendors arranged glistening tomatoes and fragrant basil with obvious pride. The pasta, rolled by hand and dressed simply with olive oil, tasted nothing like the versions served abroad. In the evenings, families gathered on terraces overlooking the sea, sharing long meals that stretched well past midnight. I learned that food, in this part of the world, is inseparable from memory, family, and the slow rhythm of the day. I will treasure those weeks for the rest of my life.

promptData.scenario: You are a member of a community sports center. The center recently changed its opening hours, and the new schedule is inconvenient for you.
promptData.direction: Write an email to the center manager. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Opening hours
goals: Describe how the new opening hours affect you.
Explain why the old schedule worked better for you.
Suggest a change to the schedule.

probes.text: Dear Ms. Palmer, The new hours are bad for me. The old hours were better. Please change them back. Thank you. Best, Sam

probes.note: 三 goal 只答两个(缺 Suggest a change)

promptData.scenario: You are a member of a community sports center. The center recently changed its opening hours, and the new schedule is inconvenient for you.
promptData.direction: Write an email to the center manager. In your email, do the following:
promptData.subject: Opening hours
goals: Describe how the new opening hours affect you.
Explain why the old schedule worked better for you.
Suggest a change to the schedule.

probes.text:
Dear Ms. Palmer,

I am writing about the recent change to the opening hours at the community sports center. The center now opens at ten in the morning and closes at six in the evening, which is very inconvenient for me.

The reason the change affects me so much is that I work a standard office job from nine to five. Under the new schedule, the center is closed before I finish work, so I can no longer use the facilities on weekdays at all. The old schedule, which kept the center open until nine in the evening, allowed me to exercise after work three or four times a week, which was very important for my health and stress levels.

Thank you for taking the time to read my email, and I hope you will consider the needs of working members.

Sincerely,
Sam

probes.note: 把一篇~4分水平文同义反复膨胀到2倍长(cal-disc-4 clean final=3.5为基线)

professor.name: Dr. Achebe
professor.text: We've been discussing whether social media has improved or harmed public debate. Some argue it gives ordinary people a voice and spreads information quickly, while others believe it amplifies misinformation and outrage. Do you think social media has been good or bad for public debate? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe social media has improved public debate because it lets people who were once ignored share their views and organize around issues they care about.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, social media has harmed debate because outrage spreads faster than facts, and algorithms reward the most extreme takes rather than the most thoughtful ones.

probes.text: I think social media has harmed public debate. Before social media, people got their news from newspapers and television, which had editors who checked facts. When social media was invented, anyone could post anything, and this changed how information spreads because false claims travel faster than corrections. I agree with Paul that algorithms reward outrage, but I think the deeper problem is that transportation of ideas now happens with no gatekeeping at all. Also social media technology helped create echo chambers where people only see views they already agree with. So I believe social media has damaged debate more than it has helped it. When social media was invented, anyone could post anything, and this changed how information spreads because false claims travel faster than corrections. I agree with Paul that algorithms reward outrage, but I think the deeper problem is that transportation of ideas now happens with no gatekeeping at all. Also social media technology helped create echo chambers where people only see views they already agree with. So I believe social media has damaged debate more than it has helped it.
